description_long: This object is a compact solar energy infrastructure unit featuring a rectangular array of photovoltaic panels mounted on a sturdy frame. The solar panels are elevated above a base structure that appears to house electrical components and possibly an inverter, with visible support struts connecting the panels to the base. The base is detailed with red and gray elements, suggesting reinforced construction and possibly access panels or control units. The overall design is modular and industrial, suitable for installation in urban environments such as rooftops, parking lots, or small utility areas. The structure is primarily composed of metal, glass, and plastic, with a sleek, functional appearance. The visible cabling and mounting hardware indicate a fixed installation intended for efficient solar energy capture and conversion.
description: A compact, modular solar panel array mounted on a reinforced base with visible support struts and electrical components. The unit features a sleek, industrial design suitable for urban installations.
description_view_0: Front view shows the solar panel array elevated above the base, with visible support struts and a central access or control unit.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the tilt of the solar panels and the detailed base structure, including red and gray components.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the back of the solar panels and the supporting frame, with less detail on the base's front-facing features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors view 1, showing the panel tilt and the arrangement of the support structure and base.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly specified, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with an aluminum frame and steel support struts
attribute_array_configuration: single row of 3 solar panels mounted side by side
